project: Cycle Control Game — AI Research Project
purpose: Complete handoff for Claude Code to continue implementation. Read this entire file before touching any code.

project_summary.what_it_is: Two-player abstract strategy game on a hex-shaped triangular grid. Score = count of your stones belonging to at least one cycle in your induced subgraph. Not territory/enclosure — cycles in graph theory sense.
project_summary.research_goal: Evaluate whether the committed ruleset produces a strategically good game under competent play, using a trained neural agent population.
project_summary.current_phase: Phase 0 preparation complete. Engine done, heuristic bots done, tournament harness done. Next: PettingZoo environment, GNN/CNN networks, PPO training.

structure.cycle_control/rules.py: RulesConfig — all rule flags, RulesConfig.committed() factory
structure.cycle_control/state.py: GameState, Player, NodeState, TurnPhase, history entries
structure.cycle_control/engine.py: MoveEngine — legality, placement, pass, undo/redo, sandbox, end conditions
structure.cycle_control/ai/action_space.py: action_index <-> node mapping, legal mask builder
structure.cycle_control/ai/bots/search_bot.py: SearchBot — alpha-beta minimax with iterative deepening
structure.tests_ai.py: 136 AI tests (includes committed ruleset + auto-fill tests)
structure.greedy_validation.py: G1 vs G2 head-to-head validation script
structure.IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES_PHASE3.md: Phase 3 findings (partially based on buggy data)
structure.IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES_PHASE4.md: Phase 4 findings — bug fix, clean R=4 data

committed_ruleset.description: The one ruleset we train on. Use RulesConfig.committed(board_radius=N).
committed_ruleset.why_these_rules: neutrality prevents parachute-blocking, strict_adjacency compresses action space for RL (tractable), mirror_adjacency adds degree-6 topology (girth 4, bipartite), no-pass prevents degenerate passing, no_legal_moves is the natural game-end condition under these rules

coordinate_system.node: (q, r, o) — axial coordinates + orientation bit (0=up-triangle, 1=down-triangle)

mirror_adjacency.note: Mirror edges connect opposite-orientation triangles across a shared vertex. Keeps graph bipartite. Degree 3->6 interior, girth 6->4.

coordinate_system.on_board: Triangle at (q,r,o) is on board iff all 3 corner vertices satisfy max(|a|,|b|,|a+b|) <= radius

turn_structure.normal: Active player places up to 2 stones per turn
turn_structure.truncated: Only 1 placement available this turn (supply or board constraints)
turn_structure.pass: DISABLED in committed ruleset. Engine still supports it for legacy/testing.
turn_structure.auto_fill: When active player has no legal moves, opponent gets the turn and auto_fill() places all reachable cells in topology order until both sides are stuck, then end_on_no_legal_moves fires.

scoring.rule: A stone scores iff it belongs to at least one simple cycle in its owner's induced subgraph
scoring.algorithm: Tarjan bridge algorithm (iterative). Node scores iff it has >= 1 non-bridge incident edge.

action_space.class: ActionSpace(topology) — precomputes node<->index mapping for speed

bot_interface.protocol: choose_action(state, legal_mask, color) -> int; reset(seed=None) -> None
bot_interface.contract: Bot MUST return index i where legal_mask[i] == True. Illegal return = bug.
bot_interface.turn_driver: play_turn(engine, state, bot, action_space) handles multi-placement turns

existing_bots.FrontierRandomBot: Random among cells adjacent to own stones
existing_bots.Greedy1: Cycle-focused one-ply: heavy cycle_score_diff, moderate component_diff. Wins 90% vs Random at R=4.
existing_bots.Greedy2: Territory-focused one-ply: heavy frontier_diff, opponent mobility penalty. Wins 100% vs G1 at R=4.

SearchBot.algorithm: Alpha-beta minimax with move ordering and iterative deepening
SearchBot.strength: Elo 1667 vs G2=1290, G1=983, Random=861 at R=4 (20 games/pair)

existing_bots.critical_bug_fixed: All bots previously chose pass when placements existed because pass evaluated as 0 while placements scored negative. Fixed: bots filter pass out of candidates whenever placements are legal.

R4_20games.key_insight: Search +377 Elo over Greedy_2 = game has real tactical depth. Zero draws = clean RL signal.

tournament_findings.discarded_data: All R=2..5 data from Phase 3 notes is based on buggy bots that passed mid-game. Only the R=4 Phase 4 data above is trustworthy.

checklist_status.section_4_2c_validation: DONE (G2 wins 92.5% vs G1 — distinct strategies confirmed)

training_plan.next_task: Section 9: PettingZoo AEC environment + observation encoder
training_plan.curriculum: R=4 -> R=7 -> R=10 (Ben-Assayag & El-Yaniv: train on small, play on large)

phase0_plan.description: Train CNN_base and GNN_base on committed ruleset, neutral reward (terminal win/loss only)
phase0_plan.split_condition: win_rate vs Greedy in 65-80%, Elo still rising, policy entropy > 50% of initial, game trajectories still varied
phase0_plan.base_models_as_artifacts: CNN_base and GNN_base checkpoints saved as permanent neutral benchmarks after split

phase1_plan.description: Clone each base into 2 style variants. 4-agent starter pool.
phase1_plan.divergence_gate: All 4 beat Greedy > 70%, cycle and territory variants play observably differently
phase1_plan.phase2_expansion: Add CNN_mid and GNN_mid (balanced shaping) only after Phase 1 divergence confirmed

GNN.input: torch_geometric.data.Data with x (node features), edge_index (from topology), batch
GNN.layers: GraphConv preferred over GCNConv (separate self-loop weight). 4-6 residual blocks, hidden_dim=128.
GNN.policy_head: Linear(hidden_dim, 1) per node -> concatenate -> append pass logit
GNN.masking: Set masked logits to -inf BEFORE softmax (not multiply probs by mask)
GNN.board_size_transfer: GNNs handle variable board size natively — edge_index just changes. Train R=4, init R=7 from R=4 checkpoint.

CNN.layout: Axial grid (H=2*R+1, W=2*R+1), two orientation channels
CNN.critical_constraint: MUST be fully convolutional — NO FC layers in feature extractor. FC layers fix board size and break curriculum transfer.
CNN.layers: AlphaZero-style ResNet: initial Conv3x3(64) + BN + ReLU, 4-6 residual blocks
CNN.masking: Same as GNN — -inf on illegal indices

architecture_plan.do_not_include_yet: Siege/territory channels — may shortcut learning. Add only if ablation shows agents aren't learning siege concepts organically.

environment_plan.multi_placement_handling: agent_selection stays on same player across both placements within a turn
environment_plan.reward: terminal only: +1 win, -1 loss, 0 draw. NO intermediate shaping in base training.
environment_plan.augmentation: 8-fold hex symmetry augmentation of observations (critical for sample efficiency)

failure_modes_to_watch.policy_collapse: entropy_loss crashes to ~0 early — increase ent_coef
failure_modes_to_watch.training_instability: approx_kl > target_kl consistently — reduce lr or n_epochs
failure_modes_to_watch.value_divergence: value_loss grows over time — increase vf_coef
failure_modes_to_watch.catastrophic_forgetting: win_rate vs older snapshots drops below 50% — increase replay window to 16+
failure_modes_to_watch.stagnation: Elo flat for 500+ iterations — increase opponent diversity
failure_modes_to_watch.masking_bug_silent: non-zero prob on masked actions — unit test before any RL run

performance_notes.game_length: ~115% board coverage at R=4. At R=10 (600 cells) expect ~690 moves/game.
performance_notes.siege_flood_fill: O(V^2) — DO NOT call per search node or greedy eval. Frontier (O(V)) is the real-time proxy.

known_decisions_and_rationale.no_passing: Pass disabled in committed ruleset. Passing hands opponent a free turn; no rational strategy should voluntarily pass when placements are legal. Auto-fill replaces the pass/end-game phase.
known_decisions_and_rationale.auto_fill_topology_order: Deterministic, no decisions, no evaluation. Fast.
known_decisions_and_rationale.end_on_no_legal_moves: Correct end condition under strict adjacency + neutrality. Empty cells may exist but be unreachable by both players, so 'no empty cells' != game-over.
known_decisions_and_rationale.frontier_not_siege: Siege flood-fill (O(V^2)) too slow for real-time eval. Frontier (O(V)) captures same strategic intent.
known_decisions_and_rationale.greedy_never_passes: Bots filter pass out when placements exist. Bug fix from Phase 4.
known_decisions_and_rationale.single_committed_ruleset: Training one ruleset only. Cross-ruleset training invalidates the value head.
known_decisions_and_rationale.GNN_first: GNN before CNN because: (1) board is a graph, (2) GNN transfers across board sizes for curriculum, (3) empirically shown to learn non-local patterns better (Ben-Assayag 2021).
known_decisions_and_rationale.no_siege_in_observation_v1: Siege channels may shortcut learning. Agents should discover siege concepts. Add only if ablation shows they aren't.

files_to_create_next: cycle_control/ai/features.py — observation encoder (graph for GNN, tensor for CNN)
cycle_control/ai/networks/gnn.py — GraphConv residual trunk + policy/value heads
cycle_control/ai/networks/cnn.py — fully convolutional ResNet trunk + policy/value heads
cycle_control/ai/training/selfplay.py — self-play wrapper, opponent bank, snapshot pool
cycle_control/ai/training/ppo_runner.py — PPO training loop, logging, checkpointing
train.py — CLI entry point: train --arch gnn --radius 4 --smoke-test

immediate_next_steps.step1: Implement cycle_control/ai/env.py — PettingZoo AEC wrapper. Validate with api_test. This is the gate for everything that follows.
immediate_next_steps.step2: Implement cycle_control/ai/features.py — observation encoder. Support both graph (Data object) and tensor (numpy array) output formats.
immediate_next_steps.step3: Implement cycle_control/ai/networks/gnn.py — forward pass test on dummy batch, shape check.
immediate_next_steps.step5: Smoke test: python train.py --arch gnn --radius 4 --smoke-test runs 100 PPO steps without crashing.
immediate_next_steps.gate: Do not start Phase 0 training until smoke test passes.

design_documents.note_on_phase3: IMPLEMENTATION_NOTES_PHASE3.md contains analysis partly based on the pass-bug-corrupted data. Cross-reference with Phase 4 notes before trusting Phase 3 conclusions.

user_context.name: Tal
user_context.background: CS student, Hebrew University Jerusalem, year 2. Strong algorithms background, fast coder.
preferences: Blunt and honest feedback preferred over diplomatic softening
State confidence levels explicitly when uncertain
English only unless explicitly asked for Hebrew
No padding or filler — direct answers
